words.definitionEn: a person or thing that is next
words.exampleEn: One moment he wasn't there, the next he was.

words.definitionEn: a place to live, work or stay in
words.exampleEn: rented/temporary accommodation

words.definitionEn: to help somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: Anyone willing to assist can contact this number.

words.definitionEn: and not
words.exampleEn: She seemed neither surprised nor worried.

words.definitionEn: and not
words.exampleEn: She seemed neither surprised nor worried.

words.definitionEn: the usual or average state, level or standard
words.exampleEn: above/below normal, The rainfall has been above normal for the time of year.

words.definitionEn: located in the north or facing north; connected with or typical of the north part of the world or a region
words.exampleEn: the northern slopes of the mountains

words.definitionEn: to notice or pay careful attention to something
words.exampleEn: note something, Note the fine early Baroque altar inside the chapel.

words.definitionEn: because the thing mentioned is happening or has just happened
words.exampleEn: Now that the kids have left home we've got a lot of extra space.

words.definitionEn: using, producing or resulting from nuclear energy
words.exampleEn: a nuclear power plant/station

words.definitionEn: easy to see or understand
words.exampleEn: I know you don't like her but try not to make it so obvious.

words.definitionEn: used when giving information that you expect other people to know already or agree with
words.exampleEn: Obviously, we don't want to spend too much money.

words.definitionEn: a particular time when something happens
words.exampleEn: on an occasion, on this/that occasion

words.definitionEn: to happen
words.exampleEn: When exactly did the incident occur?

words.definitionEn: strange or unusual
words.exampleEn: They're very odd people.

words.definitionEn: agreed to, said, done, etc. by somebody who is in a position of authority
words.exampleEn: an official announcement/decision/statement

words.definitionEn: not modern; no longer fashionable
words.exampleEn: old-fashioned clothes/styles/methods/equipment

words.definitionEn: as soon as; when
words.exampleEn: We didn't know how we would cope once the money had gone.

words.definitionEn: the process of cutting open a part of a person’s body in order to remove or repair a damaged part
words.exampleEn: Will I need to have an operation?

words.definitionEn: the mixture of gases that surrounds the earth
words.exampleEn: Wind power doesn't release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.

words.definitionEn: involving large numbers of people who work together to do something in a way that has been carefully planned
words.exampleEn: an organized body of workers

words.definitionEn: a person who makes the arrangements for something
words.exampleEn: the organizers of the festival

words.definitionEn: a document, work of art, etc. produced for the first time, from which copies are later made
words.exampleEn: This painting is a copy; the original is in Madrid.

words.definitionEn: used to describe the situation that existed at the beginning of a particular period or activity, especially before something was changed
words.exampleEn: The school was originally very small.

words.definitionEn: to fasten or join one thing to another
words.exampleEn: attach something, I attach a copy of my notes for your information.

words.definitionEn: used to say what is the right thing to do
words.exampleEn: They ought to apologize.

words.definitionEn: the one or ones that belong to us
words.exampleEn: Their house is very similar to ours, but ours is bigger.

words.definitionEn: used, happening or located outside rather than in a building
words.exampleEn: outdoor pursuits/recreation/activities

words.definitionEn: outside, rather than in a building
words.exampleEn: The rain prevented them from eating outdoors.

words.definitionEn: a container, usually made of paper, that holds a number of the same thing or an amount of something, ready to be sold
words.exampleEn: pack of something, a pack of cigarettes/gum

words.definitionEn: something that is wrapped in paper or put into a thick envelope so that it can be sent by mail, carried easily, or given as a present
words.exampleEn: A large package has arrived for you.

words.definitionEn: causing you pain
words.exampleEn: Is your back still painful?

words.definitionEn: having skin that is very light in colour; having skin that has less colour than usual because of illness, a strong emotion, etc.
words.exampleEn: a pale complexion

words.definitionEn: to take part in or become involved in an activity
words.exampleEn: Anyone who wishes to participate is welcome.

words.definitionEn: especially; more than usual or more than others
words.exampleEn: particularly useful/important/interesting

words.definitionEn: an official document or ticket that shows that you have the right to enter or leave a place, to travel on a bus or train, etc.
words.exampleEn: a boarding pass (= for a plane)

words.definitionEn: a very strong feeling of love, hate, anger, enthusiasm, etc.
words.exampleEn: I admire the passion and commitment shown by the players.

words.definitionEn: the way that you think and feel about somebody/something; the way that you behave towards somebody/something that shows how you think and feel
words.exampleEn: attitude towards somebody/something, These societies have to change their attitudes towards women.

words.definitionEn: a way or track that is built or is made by the action of people walking
words.exampleEn: a dirt/gravel/concrete path

words.definitionEn: if you are attracted by something, it interests you and makes you want it; if you are attracted by somebody, you like or admire them
words.exampleEn: be attracted by somebody/something, I had always been attracted by the idea of working abroad.

words.definitionEn: the act of paying somebody/something or of being paid
words.exampleEn: What method of payment do you prefer?

words.definitionEn: not involving a war, violence or argument
words.exampleEn: a peaceful protest/demonstration

words.definitionEn: an interesting or lively place to go or thing to do
words.exampleEn: Buckingham Palace is a major tourist attraction.

words.definitionEn: the number, amount or rate of something, expressed as if it is part of a total that is 100; a part or share of a whole
words.exampleEn: percentage of something/somebody, What percentage of the population is/are overweight?

words.definitionEn: completely
words.exampleEn: It's perfectly normal to feel like this.

words.definitionEn: the act of performing a play, concert or some other form of entertainment
words.exampleEn: The performance starts at seven.

words.definitionEn: used to show that you are giving your own opinion about something
words.exampleEn: Personally, I prefer the second option.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody do something by giving them good reasons for doing it
words.exampleEn: persuade somebody to do something, Try to persuade him to come.

words.definitionEn: a person who takes photographs, especially as a job
words.exampleEn: a wedding/wildlife/fashion photographer

words.definitionEn: the art, process or job of taking photographs or filming something
words.exampleEn: Her hobbies include hiking and photography.

words.definitionEn: a short thin piece of stiff wire with a sharp point at one end and a round head at the other, used especially for fastening together pieces of cloth when sewing
words.exampleEn: Use pins to keep the patch in place while you sew it on.

words.definitionEn: to attach something onto another thing or fasten things together with a pin, etc.
words.exampleEn: She pinned the badge onto her jacket.

words.definitionEn: a tube through which liquids and gases can flow
words.exampleEn: Both hot and cold water pipes should be properly insulated.

words.definitionEn: to put something in a particular place, especially when you do it carefully or deliberately
words.exampleEn: He placed his hand on her shoulder.

words.definitionEn: the act or process of making plans for something
words.exampleEn: The department is responsible for all financial planning.

words.definitionEn: fun, attractive, or giving pleasure
words.exampleEn: a pleasant evening/atmosphere/walk

words.definitionEn: a state of feeling or being happy or satisfied
words.exampleEn: His eyes lit up with pleasure.

words.definitionEn: a large amount; as much or as many as you need
words.exampleEn: plenty of eggs/money/time

words.definitionEn: the series of events that form the story of a novel, play, film, etc.
words.exampleEn: It's hard to follow the plot of the film.

words.definitionEn: used when the two numbers or amounts mentioned are being added together
words.exampleEn: Two plus five is seven.

words.definitionEn: a piece of writing in which the words are chosen for their sound and the images they suggest, not just for their obvious meanings. The words are arranged in separate lines, usually with a repeated rhythm, and often the lines rhyme at the end.
words.exampleEn: His collected poems were published after the war.

words.definitionEn: a person who writes poems
words.exampleEn: an internationally renowned poet

words.definitionEn: poems in general; poems as a genre of literature
words.exampleEn: lyric/epic poetry

words.definitionEn: to stretch out your finger or something held in your hand towards somebody/something in order to show somebody where a person or thing is
words.exampleEn: point at somebody/something, ‘What's your name?’ he asked, pointing at the child with his pen.

words.definitionEn: a substance that causes death or harm if it gets into the body
words.exampleEn: Some mushrooms contain a deadly poison.

words.definitionEn: to harm or kill a person or an animal by giving them poison
words.exampleEn: poison somebody/yourself, He was believed to poison his enemies.

words.definitionEn: causing death or illness if swallowed or taken into the body
words.exampleEn: poisonous chemicals/fumes/plants

words.definitionEn: the power to give orders to people
words.exampleEn: in a position of authority

words.definitionEn: a plan of action agreed or chosen by a political party, a business, etc.
words.exampleEn: policy on something, the present government’s policy on education

words.definitionEn: connected with the state, government or public affairs
words.exampleEn: a monarch without political power

words.definitionEn: a person whose job involves politics, especially as an elected member of parliament, etc.
words.exampleEn: democratically elected politicians

words.definitionEn: the activities involved in getting and using power in public life, and being able to influence decisions that affect a country or a society
words.exampleEn: world/international politics

words.definitionEn: a town or city with a harbour, especially one where ships load and unload goods
words.exampleEn: a container/fishing ports

words.definitionEn: a painting, drawing or photograph of a person, especially of the head and shoulders
words.exampleEn: portrait of somebody, a portrait of his wife

words.definitionEn: used to say that something might exist, happen or be true, but you are not certain
words.exampleEn: It was possibly their worst performance ever.

words.definitionEn: a deep round container used for cooking things in
words.exampleEn: pots and pans

words.definitionEn: to make a liquid or other substance flow from a container in a continuous stream, especially by holding the container at an angle
words.exampleEn: pour something + adv./prep., Pour the sauce over the pasta.

words.definitionEn: the state of being poor
words.exampleEn: conditions of extreme/abject poverty

words.definitionEn: a dry mass of very small fine pieces or grains
words.exampleEn: milk/chilli/cocoa powder

words.definitionEn: being able to control and influence people and events
words.exampleEn: an incredibly powerful organization

words.definitionEn: connected with real situations rather than with ideas or theories
words.exampleEn: to have gained practical experience of the work

words.definitionEn: to speak to God, especially to give thanks or ask for help
words.exampleEn: They knelt down and prayed.

words.definitionEn: words that you say to God giving thanks or asking for help
words.exampleEn: to say your prayers

words.definitionEn: a statement that says what you think will happen; the act of making such a statement
words.exampleEn: The results of the experiment confirmed our predictions.

words.definitionEn: ready and able to deal with something
words.exampleEn: We'll be better prepared next time.

words.definitionEn: a meeting at which something, especially a new product or idea, or piece of work, is shown to a group of people
words.exampleEn: presentation on/about somebody/something, The sales manager will give a presentation on the new products.

words.definitionEn: newspapers and magazines
words.exampleEn: the local/national/foreign press

words.definitionEn: to push part of a device, etc. in order to make it work
words.exampleEn: press something, to press a button/key

words.definitionEn: the act of trying to persuade or to force somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: pressure for something, The pressure for change continued to mount.

words.definitionEn: to behave in a particular way, in order to make other people believe something that is not true
words.exampleEn: I'm tired of having to pretend all the time.

words.definitionEn: to be equal to a particular amount as an average
words.exampleEn: Economic growth is expected to average 2% next year.

words.definitionEn: happening or existing before the event or object that you are talking about
words.exampleEn: No previous experience is necessary for this job.

words.definitionEn: at a time before the time that you are talking about
words.exampleEn: The building had previously been used as a hotel.

words.definitionEn: a person who is qualified to perform religious duties and ceremonies in the Roman Catholic, Anglican and Orthodox Churches
words.exampleEn: a parish priest

words.definitionEn: connected with the education of children between the ages of about five and eleven
words.exampleEn: primary teachers

words.definitionEn: a male member of a royal family who is not king, especially the son or grandson of the king or queen
words.exampleEn: the royal princes

words.definitionEn: a female member of a royal family who is not a queen, especially the daughter or granddaughter of the king or queen
words.exampleEn: the royal princesses

words.definitionEn: the act of producing letters, pictures, patterns, etc. on something by pressing a surface covered with ink (= coloured liquid for printing, drawing, etc.) against it
words.exampleEn: the invention of printing

words.definitionEn: a person who is kept in prison as a punishment, or while they are waiting for trial
words.exampleEn: The number of prisoners serving life sentences has fallen.

words.definitionEn: belonging to or for the use of a particular person or group; not for public use
words.exampleEn: The sign said, ‘Private property. Keep out.’

words.definitionEn: to make an official decision to give something to somebody as a payment, prize, contract, etc.
words.exampleEn: award something, Knowing why and how corporations award contracts is vitally important.

words.definitionEn: a person, a company or a country that grows or makes food, goods or materials
words.exampleEn: French wine producers

words.definitionEn: the process of growing or making food, goods or materials, especially large quantities
words.exampleEn: food/oil production

words.definitionEn: knowing or realizing something
words.exampleEn: As you're aware, this is not a new problem.

words.definitionEn: a type of job that needs special training or skill, especially one that needs a high level of education
words.exampleEn: the medical/legal/teaching profession

words.definitionEn: the money that you make in business or by selling things, especially after paying the costs involved
words.exampleEn: annual/pre-tax/corporate profits

words.definitionEn: to give a computer, etc. a set of instructions to make it perform a particular task
words.exampleEn: In this class, students will learn how to program.

words.definitionEn: to help sell a product, service, etc. or make it more popular by advertising it or offering it at a special price
words.exampleEn: promote something, The band has gone on tour to promote their new album.

words.definitionEn: right, appropriate or correct; according to the rules
words.exampleEn: We should have had a proper discussion before voting.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is correct and/or appropriate
words.exampleEn: How much money do we need to do the job properly?

words.definitionEn: a thing or things that are owned by somebody; a possession or possessions
words.exampleEn: personal/stolen property

words.definitionEn: the expression of strong dislike of or opposition to something; a statement or an action that shows this
words.exampleEn: to hold/organize/join a protest

words.definitionEn: to say or do something to show that you disagree with something or think it is bad, especially publicly
words.exampleEn: There's no use protesting, I won't change my mind.

words.definitionEn: feeling pleased and satisfied about something that you own or have done, or are connected with
words.exampleEn: proud parents

words.definitionEn: to use facts, evidence, etc. to show that something is true
words.exampleEn: prove something, They hope this new evidence will prove her innocence.

words.definitionEn: an act of trying to make something move by taking hold of it and using force to bring it towards you
words.exampleEn: I gave the door a sharp pull and it opened.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody suffer because they have broken the law or done something wrong
words.exampleEn: punish somebody, Those responsible for this crime will be severely punished.

words.definitionEn: an act or a way of punishing somebody
words.exampleEn: to inflict/impose/mete out punishment

words.definitionEn: an act of pushing something/somebody
words.exampleEn: She gave him a gentle push.

words.definitionEn: an exam that you have passed or a course of study that you have successfully completed
words.exampleEn: academic/educational/professional/vocational qualifications

words.definitionEn: having passed the exams or completed the training that are necessary in order to do a particular job; having the experience to do a particular job
words.exampleEn: a qualified teacher

words.definitionEn: to reach the standard of ability or knowledge needed to do a particular job, for example by completing a course of study or passing exams
words.exampleEn: How long does it take to qualify?

words.definitionEn: a line of people, cars, etc. waiting for something or to do something
words.exampleEn: There were long queues at polling stations.

words.definitionEn: to wait in a line of people, vehicles, etc. in order to do something, get something or go somewhere
words.exampleEn: queue (up), People queued up outside.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement that somebody has with a bank, etc. to keep money there, take some out, etc.
words.exampleEn: I don't have a bank account.

words.definitionEn: to leave your job, school, etc.
words.exampleEn: If I don't get more money I'll quit.

words.definitionEn: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful
words.exampleEn: a dictionary of quotations

words.definitionEn: a group of words or a short piece of writing taken from a book, play, speech, etc. and repeated because it is interesting or useful
words.exampleEn: The essay was full of quotes.

words.definitionEn: to repeat the exact words that another person has said or written
words.exampleEn: quote something, to quote Shakespeare

words.definitionEn: the sport of racing horses
words.exampleEn: He used to watch the racing on TV in the afternoons.

words.definitionEn: towards a place or position that is behind
words.exampleEn: I lost my balance and fell backwards.

words.definitionEn: a variety of things of a particular type
words.exampleEn: range of something, The hotel offers a wide range of facilities.

words.definitionEn: not done, seen, happening, etc. very often
words.exampleEn: a rare disease/occurrence/event

words.definitionEn: not very often
words.exampleEn: She is rarely seen in public nowadays.
